description:
Context:
    You are a performance engineer who has just received a raw profiling dump produced by an
    instrumented build of an application.  
    Everything you need is already on-disk in the directory

        /home/user/app_profiling

    Two files are present there **before you start**:

      1. /home/user/app_profiling/raw_profile.json  
         • A JSON array where each element describes one instrumented function.  
         • For every element the following keys exist:  
           – "function"  : string – fully-qualified function name  
           – "calls"     : integer – how many times it was invoked  
           – "avg_ms"    : number  – mean time (milliseconds) spent per call  

      2. /home/user/app_profiling/profile_schema.json  
         • A JSON-Schema Draft-07 document that formally defines the structure shown above.

    Your tasks are:

      A. Verify that raw_profile.json conforms to profile_schema.json.  
         • Log the result to the file  
             /home/user/app_profiling/validation.log  
         • validation.log must contain **exactly one line** (no newline at the end) with one of
           these two literal strings:
               validation: OK
               validation: FAIL

      B. Produce an easy-to-read “hot-spots” extraction.  
         • Using jq, create the file  
             /home/user/app_profiling/hotspots.json  
         • hotspots.json must contain a JSON array (no pretty-printing needed) holding only
           those entries from raw_profile.json whose "avg_ms" value is strictly greater than 10.  
         • Keep only the "function" and "avg_ms" keys for every retained entry.  
         • Sort the resulting array in **descending** order of "avg_ms".  
         • Example of the exact required format (spacing is insignificant, order of keys inside
           each object must follow the example):  
           [
             {"function":"some.ns.topFunction","avg_ms":22.5},
             {"function":"other.ns.worker","avg_ms":14.1}
           ]

    Constraints:
    • Finish everything using at most 2-3 well-chosen terminal commands.  
    • Do not alter raw_profile.json or profile_schema.json.  
    • Do not create any extra files other than validation.log and hotspots.json.  
    • Both new files must be located directly inside /home/user/app_profiling and be world-readable.
    • Any tool you use must already be available in a standard Debian/Ubuntu environment;
      jq is installed, but you do NOT have root access.

    The automated grader will:
      1. Make sure validation.log contains exactly one of the required strings with no trailing
         newline or extra whitespace.
      2. Parse hotspots.json and confirm that:
         • It is valid JSON.
         • It contains only the correctly filtered and ordered subset.
         • Each object has exactly two keys in the correct order.

    Complete the task via the terminal prompt.
truth:
# Initial on-disk state
    The container starts with the following directory tree and file contents:

    /home/user/app_profiling/
    ├── profile_schema.json
    └── raw_profile.json

    1. /home/user/app_profiling/profile_schema.json
       ---------------------------------------------
       {
         "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",
         "type": "array",
         "items": {
           "type": "object",
           "required": ["function", "calls", "avg_ms"],
           "additionalProperties": false,
           "properties": {
             "function": { "type": "string" },
             "calls":    { "type": "integer", "minimum": 0 },
             "avg_ms":   { "type": "number",  "minimum": 0 }
           }
         },
         "minItems": 1
       }

    2. /home/user/app_profiling/raw_profile.json
       -----------------------------------------
       [
         { "function": "core.init",        "calls": 1,   "avg_ms": 5.0  },
         { "function": "engine.render",    "calls": 90,  "avg_ms": 12.4 },
         { "function": "engine.update",    "calls": 90,  "avg_ms": 15.8 },
         { "function": "engine.physics",   "calls": 90,  "avg_ms": 7.1  },
         { "function": "core.cleanup",     "calls": 1,   "avg_ms": 3.2  }
       ]

    # Files the agent must create
    After successful completion, **both** of these files must exist:

    a) /home/user/app_profiling/validation.log  
       Expected content (no trailing newline):
           validation: OK

    b) /home/user/app_profiling/hotspots.json  
       Expected content (minified JSON, key order as shown):
           [{"function":"engine.update","avg_ms":15.8},{"function":"engine.render","avg_ms":12.4}]
